scope_note: Final 5-GPU dataset (Ampere → Ada → Hopper → Blackwell). T4 (Turing) was dropped after recurrent Modal worker stability issues; H200 (Hopper+) was dropped after a measurement anomaly on gpt2-large. Decode skips encoder-only models (BERT family); T5 enc-dec decode is TODO.

data.config_grid: 8 batches × 5 seqs × 3 models × 5 GPUs (seq=1024 skipped on decode mode for next-token headroom)

comparison_to_prefill_5gpu.note: Decode-mode prediction is *easier* than prefill for graph-aware models. Decode latency is dominated by reading model weights (memory-bound, very repetitive across configs); GNN with sum-readout + per-node features captures the per-op-bytes signature directly. Prefill latency mixes compute and memory regimes which adds noise.

cross_gpu_observations: gpt2-small/medium/large per-token decode latencies scale ~3× from small to large across all 5 GPUs (vs ~6.2× param ratio) — bandwidth-saturated, not compute-saturated
B200 vs H100: ~2× faster on decode, tracking the 2.4× HBM bandwidth ratio (8 TB/s vs 3.35 TB/s) — clean memory-bound signature

A100-SXM4-40GB anomalously slower than A10 / L4 on decode despite 2.6× more bandwidth — likely eager-mode attention dispatch quirk on Ampere SM_80 (we use attn_implementation='eager' per scope decision)
